1: Google ADK has a number of very useful pre-built tools such as Google Search,
2: Code Execution and Vertex AI Search Tool.
4: The Tool Use pattern is a critical architectural principle for extending the functional
5: scope of large language models beyond their intrinsic text generation capabilities. By
6: equipping a model with the ability to interface with external software and data
7: sources, this paradigm allows an agent to perform actions, execute computations,
8: and retrieve information from other systems. This process involves the model
9: generating a structured request to call an external tool when it determines that doing
10: so is necessary to fulfill a user's query. Frameworks such as LangChain, Google ADK,
11: and Crew AI offer structured abstractions and components that facilitate the
12: integration of these external tools. These frameworks manage the process of
13: exposing tool specifications to the model and parsing its subsequent tool-use
14: requests. This simplifies the development of sophisticated agentic systems that can
15: interact with and take action within external digital environments.
